title: melampyrum arvense f. pseudobarbatum
intro: Melampyrum arvense f. pseudobarbatum, also known as the yellow bird's tongue, is a rare and unique variety of the Melampyrum arvense plant, belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. It is an parasitic perennial herb with medicinal properties, containing compounds like melampine and pseudopelletierine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Melampyrum arvense f. pseudobarbatum is typically used as an infusion or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to create remedies for its reported health benefits.
